title: ericameria parishii
intro: Ericameria parishii, also known as Parish's goldenrod or Parish's rabbitbrush, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ericameria parishii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to make teas and topical creams for medicinal purposes.
